newsSince: On June 18 SPX announced >$100M of HVAC manufacturing capacity expansion plus new data-center cooling products (Marley OlympusMAX fluid cooler), with management guiding ~50% data-center sales growth in 2026 and HVAC backlog up ~38% organically; the stock rose ~5%. The June 9 Wells Fargo conference reinforced the data-center cooling narrative. No M&A, earnings restatement, or guidance cut.

sources.label: SPXC up 5.2% on HVAC capacity expansion (Simply Wall St)
SPXC clearer data-center cooling play as backlog builds (Insider Monkey/Yahoo)

verdicts.rankRationale: Latent lens rewards undiscovered supply-chain exposure. June 18 capacity expansion + DC cooling product launch and ~50% 2026 DC growth confirm real exposure, but the explicit data-center framing accelerates discovery, capping latent upside. Net mild positive, no peer re-rank.
verdicts.bull: DC cooling demand real; >$100M capacity add and +38% organic HVAC backlog validate the thesis.
verdicts.neutral: Exposure confirmed but increasingly consensus; cooling angle now widely modeled.
verdicts.bear: Discovery erodes the 'latent' premium; thermal is competitive vs purpose-built liquid-cooling peers.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Data-center HVAC backlog conversion and ~50% 2026 DC sales growth.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Loss of latency/discovery edge as the cooling story goes consensus.
